stories.title: Pentagon likely to cancel missile sale to Germany over fears of Russia
stories.summary: The Pentagon is expected to cancel a plan to send Tomahawk missiles to Germany partly because officials are concerned Russia will view it as an escalation, a startling reversal of a long-planned agreement with one of America’s biggest allies. U.S. officials fear Moscow will retaliate if the Trump administration follows through on the effort to deploy precision missiles in the middle of the continent, according to two European officials and one American official. But any decision not to deliver t

stories.title: Europe’s far right exploit Henry Nowak murder in UK with populist rhetoric on race
stories.summary: Polish, Spanish and French populists focus on clips of teenager’s dying moments and accuse UK of descending ‘into depths of the earth’ UK politics live – latest updates Polish far-right politicians have claimed that the murder of Henry Nowak symbolises “Britain’s descent into the depths of the earth” as populists from France, Spain and Japan focused on harrowing clips of his dying moments. Despite pleas from Nowak’s family for people not to exploit the killing for political gain and to focus on

stories.title: Mexico’s ex-president accuses US of plotting to weaken governing party
stories.summary: Andrés Manuel López Obrador says Washington is using investigations into governors and propaganda to boost rivals Mexico’s former president, Andrés Manuel López Obrador , has accused US officials of trying to weaken the governing party to strengthen the opposition, amid rising tensions between the two countries over Washington’s investigations into several Mexican governors. “Some US officials are plotting to weaken Morena and strengthen the rightwing opposition in Mexico with the aim of restori

stories.title: John Bolton reaches plea deal over mishandling classified documents
stories.summary: Justice department filed charges against Trump’s former adviser in 2025 as part of onslaught against president’s critics US politics live – latest updates Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email John Bolton, the former US national security adviser who left Donald Trump ’s first administration and became a staunch critic of the US president, has reached a plea agreement in a case criminally charging him with mishandling classified documents, according to a source familiar with the matte

stories.title: Romania set for technocratic government after parties fail to end feud
stories.summary: Romania’s president has appointed a Member of European Parliament to lead a technocratic government in a bid to end weeks of political disarray and economic anxiety following the collapse of the country’s centrist coalition. President Nicușor Dan designated the liberal Renew Europe MEP Eugen Tomac as prime minister, on the grounds that none of the party leaders in Romania’s fractious parliament could form a stable administration. That, the president said, left him no option but to bring in

stories.title: Charges dropped against Budapest mayor over 2025 Pride march
stories.summary: Budapest's liberal Mayor Gergely Karacsony had been charged with helping to organise the banned 2025 march.

stories.title: Szijjártó alatt már meg sem lepődött senki, hogy nincs, Orbán Anita most 15 év után visszahozza
stories.summary: A legtöbb országban a külügyminisztériumok céljait és prioritásait részletesen kidolgozott stratégiákban szokás összefoglalni. 2011-ben még Orbán alatt is készült nyilvánosan elérhető külpolitikai stratégia, mai szemmel meglepő alapvetésekkel. Szijjártó alatt hűlt helye volt, de a Magyar-kormány újból lefekteti, mik alapján képzeli el a nemzetközi kapcsolatokat és az ország helyét a világban.

stories.title: Optimizmus vagy végkiárusítás: miért vesznek fel ilyen eszméletlen sok hitelt a magyarok?
stories.summary: Megduplázódtak a lakáshitelek, már négy hónap alatt ezer milliárd forintot vettek fel belőlük – derül ki a Magyar Nemzeti Bank (MNB) friss adataiból.

stories.title: Először igazolták az afrikai sertéspestist Magyarországon házi sertésekben
stories.summary: Az országos főállatorvos azonnali hatósági intézkedéseket rendelt el.

stories.title: Megbuherálta a rendszerét, évekig fékezte a propaganda megjelenését a Hírkereső tulajdonosa
stories.summary: Holló Gábor azt állítja: több közvélemény-kutatást is finanszírozott a Tisza Pártnak, és összesen nagyjából 50 millió forinttal támogatta Magyarékat.

stories.title: Pimasznak és illegálisnak tartja az ukrán jegybank a pénzszállítók elleni akciót, amiről Orbán Viktor döntött
stories.summary: Az ukrán jegybank a Telex tényfeltáró cikkére reagált. Azért is bíznak a szigorú fellépésben, mert meg vannak győződve arról, hogy a jogállamiságnak az EU alapvető értékének kell maradnia.

stories.title: Az évtizedes és sokmilliárdos parkfenntartási bizniszhez vezettek az Őrsi Gergelyt és fideszes politikusokat érintő korrupciós ügy szálai
stories.summary: Már 2009-ben GVH-vizsgálat indult a budapesti parkfenntartási piac szereplőivel szemben, de az ágazat működése se a 2010-es, se a 2019-es politikai fordulat után nem változott meg. Az ügyészség szerint az egyik parkfenntartó cég több milliárd forint kenőpénzt osztott vissza fideszes, MSZP-s és momentumos politikusoknak.

stories.title: Magyar Péter azt kívánja, legyen sokkal kényelmesebb az írek uniós elnöksége, mint a magyarok elnöksége volt
stories.summary: Magyar Péter az ír miniszterelnökkel tárgyalt Budapesten.

stories.title: „Győzött a szabadság” – civil szervezetek reagáltak arra, hogy ejtették a vádat a pécsi és a budapesti Pride szervezői ellen
stories.summary: A közleményt kiadó civil szervezetek azt írták, 2025-től kezdve közösen dolgoztak azért, hogy visszaállítsák a gyülekezési jog szabad gyakorlását.

stories.title: Emlékszik még a januári rezsistopra? Az MVM biztos szívesen elfelejtené már
stories.summary: Rég vége már a télnek, de a közműcég még a nyár elején is az összevissza érkező számlák miatt kénytelen magyarázkodni a semmiből a nyakába zúdított kampányajándék jóvoltából.

stories.title: Hungary’s new foreign policy strategy is taking shape, and it couldn’t differ more from what we’ve seen in the past fifteen years
stories.summary: In most countries, the foreign policy goals and priorities are typically summarized in detailed, publicly available documents. Under Orbán, 2011 was the last time such a strategy was drafted for Hungary. Szijjártó didn't bother with it, but now, the new Hungarian government is establishing a new vision for the country's place in the world.

stories.title: Sulyok szerint sürgős eljárás keretében vizsgálja a Velencei Bizottság, hogy a Tisza állami tisztségviselőket akar lemondatni
stories.summary: A köztársasági elnök Facebook-posztjából az is kiderült: az Európa Tanács alkotmányjogi tanácsadó testületének munkatársai készek Magyarországra utazni, hogy felmérjék a helyzetet.

stories.title: A Fidesz a padlón, de Magyar Péter még ott is üti
stories.summary: Ha csak a Facebookot nézzük, a Fidesz most rosszabbul áll, mint az óellenzék a 2022-es kiütéses választási vereség után. Orbán a közösségi médiában is teljes sorcserét vezényel le, miközben Vitézy Dávid körül vonatkultusz épül, Hadházynak meg visszavonultatja a mezszámát az NBA. Lájkbajnokság.

stories.title: Brazen and illegal – National Bank of Ukraine on raid against bank vehicles ordered by Orbán
stories.summary: The National Bank of Ukraine reacted to Telex’s investigative article and said they were confident there would be a firm response from the authorities, as the rule of law must remain a fundamental value of the EU.

stories.title: Az ukrán külügyminiszter és az Európai Bizottság is üdvözli a Magyarország és Ukrajna között zajló tárgyalásokat
stories.summary: „Új fejezetet nyitunk az ukrán–magyar kapcsolatokban – egy olyan fejezetet, amely a kölcsönös tiszteleten, a bizalmon és közös európai jövőnkön alapul” – írta Andrij Szibiha.

stories.title: Hazánk mellett Spanyolország lesz a kínai autóipar másik európai hídfőállása
stories.summary: Bár korábban felröppent, hogy az MG márkát is birtokló SAIC Motor Magyarországon építene gyárat, végül a spanyolországi Galícia tartományt választotta.

stories.title: Rezsicsökkentéssel nem lehet bemenni az eurózónába
stories.summary: „10 év után visszatértünk oda, hogy a lengyel gazdaság legyen a viszonyítási pont”, mondták az Amundi Alapkezelő elemzői csütörtökön. A geopolitikai helyzetelemzés mellett a magyar gazdaság kegyelmi állapotának tartósságáról is szó esett.

stories.title: Ismét felfüggesztették a Flydubai budapesti járatait
stories.summary: A légitársaság budapesti járatai februárban álltak le, majd május végén váltak újra elérhetővé.

stories.title: Valóban áttörést hozhattak a kárpátaljai magyarok jogairól szóló megbeszélések Ukrajnával
stories.summary: Egyre valószínűbb, hogy Magyar Péter és Volodimir Zelenszkij már a jövő héten találkozhat. A kisebbségi jogok biztosítása után Ukrajna előtt megnyílhat az EU-csatlakozási tárgyalás első fejezete.

stories.title: Valós időben látszik egy videón, ahogy a Hold lenyugszik a Citadella mögött
stories.summary: A felhők közül előbukkanó Hold néhány percre a Szabadság-szobor és a Citadella zászlaja mellé került egy budapesti hajnali felvételen. A több mint hétperces videó valós időben mutatja az együttállást.

stories.title: Illegális adó kivezetésével kerül el uniós pénzből levonható büntetést a magyar állam
stories.summary: A 24.hu értesülése alapján már nem kell befizetni a kiegészítő bányajáradékot, amely uniós jogot sért, de történetesen nem jött volna rosszul NER-es felvásárlások elősegítéséhez.

stories.title: Orbán nem számolt a realitással az aranykonvoj-ügyben
stories.summary: Tényszerű Orbán Viktor felelőssége a rajtaütésben, amikor a TEK-esek ukrán pénszállítókat fogtak el, és több milliárdnyi pénzt és aranyat foglaltak le – valójában politikai célból. Mi lehetett Orbán fejében, és mi volt ebben a történetben a titkosszolgálatok és az ügyészség szerepe? Dezső András a rajtaütés hátteréről mesélt a Telex stúdiójában.

stories.title: A botok által generált internetes forgalom már meghaladta az emberekét
stories.summary: A Cloudflare vezérigazgatója ezt a fordulatot eredetileg 2027-re várta.

stories.title: Örülhetnek a Sameday-károsultak, kivonul a cég Magyarországról
stories.summary: Nem sikerült eladni a magyarországi leánycéget, kivonulnak.

stories.title: The EU should fast-track Ukraine’s membership of the club – it has the most to gain | Mujtaba Rahman
stories.summary: Many EU governments oppose it, but the strategic advantages of swift admission could be immense Russia’s war on Ukraine is now in its fifth year and a ceasefire remains elusive. The US’s attention is divided, limiting external pressure for compromise, while Moscow and Kyiv both still believe they can strengthen their respective negotiating positions through battlefield gains. At some point, however, a deal will have to be done. The parameters of that deal are already understood by negotiators on

stories.title: What the Hellenic! Why is Christopher Nolan’s new Greek epic entirely devoid of Greeks?
stories.summary: Set to be this year’s biggest blockbuster, The Odyssey’s cast has been selected to ‘represent the world’. Fair enough – except that one key country seems to have gone completely unrepresented … There are the American accents, gleaming body suits and a muddy Dunkirk palette. And then there is Lupita Nyong’o as Helen of Troy, a casting choice that recently drew racist attacks from the usual moaners of the internet , including Elon Musk, who complained it wasn’t authentic. Authenticity matters. He’

stories.title: Canada calls for US and Mexico free trade deal to be renewed for 16 years
stories.summary: “The Agreement is highly beneficial to each of our countries and to the integrated North American economy," a letter conveying Canada’s recommendation, sent to the US and Mexico trade representatives, said.

stories.title: Experts criticise plan for American-only Ebola quarantine centre in Kenya
stories.summary: Plan departs from policy of bringing CDC staff back to US for treatment and offering support to all health workers Former top US officials and other experts are urging the Trump administration to abandon plans for an Ebola quarantine and treatment centre in Kenya, as the union for workers with the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) calls for Americans exposed to Ebola to be brought home for treatment. Soon after the US revealed it was setting up a field hospital in Kenya for the

stories.title: Students clash with police in Brussels over education budget cuts
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — A student protest in Brussels turned violent on Thursday as demonstrators clashed with police in the city center over a controversial education reform. Teachers had been protesting education budget cuts in Belgium’s French-speaking regions since the beginning of the week, with students joining the demonstrations Thursday morning. Videos and photos published on social media showed protesters setting e-scooters on fire and smashing the windows of shops and bus stops near Brussels-

stories.title: EU sues Ireland over failure to protect carbon-rich bogs
stories.summary: DUBLIN — The European Commission is taking Ireland to court over its failure to protect environmentally crucial boglands from commercial turf-cutters. Today’s move — particularly embarrassing for Ireland, given it’s taking over the EU Council presidency in July — follows a damning report from its own Environmental Protection Agency that documented widespread flouting of EU laws on protecting bogs. The Commission said in a statement that it is referring Ireland to the Court of Justice of the Euro

stories.title: EU trade chief wants to emulate US intensity in talks with China
stories.summary: PARIS — EU Trade Commissioner Maroš Šefčovič wants to take a page from Washington’s playbook in trade talks with China. After meeting with Chinese trade envoy Li Chenggang in Paris on Thursday, the bloc’s trade chief told reporters that the EU will engage in deeper negotiations with Chinese authorities to resolve “what is becoming an unsustainable trade deficit with China.” Šefčovič suggested the bloc might draw inspiration from the groundwork done by the U.S. admini

stories.title: Top Trump official slams Germany (again) over verdict for man who called Merz a liar
stories.summary: Senior U.S. diplomat Sarah Rogers accused Berlin of censorship, slamming Germany’s free speech laws over the case of a German citizen who was convicted and fined for referring to Chancellor Friedrich Merz as Lügenfritz — or “lying Fritz.” “An argument I often hear is, ‘given Germany’s unique history, shouldn’t we grant it some deference to censor?’ Here’s the kind of thing German censorship extends to,” Rogers posted on X on Thursday, attaching a screenshot of a media report about the case. The

stories.title: Protesters clash with police in Albania over Jared Kushner-backed luxury resort – video
stories.summary: Protests in Albania over a proposed luxury resort backed by Jared Kushner , Donald Trump’s son-in-law, are expected to intensify after opponents rejected an offer from the country’s prime minister 'to discuss solutions'. Footage showed police firing water cannon at protesters as thousands of people took to the streets of Tirana for a third day on Wednesday, some of them holding inflatable flamingos to symbolise the environmental damage they fear the project could cause, amid growing calls for it

stories.title: Civilians flee as Somali troops and opposition-allied militias trade fire in Mogadishu
stories.summary: Violence flares before protests on Thursday over president’s decision to remain in office after his term expired Fierce clashes have taken place between government troops and militias allied with the opposition in Somalia’s capital, Mogadishu, damaging property and forcing some civilians to flee. In the runup to the fighting, which started on Wednesday afternoon, opposition leaders embedded with militias set up positions in their clan strongholds the city. Continue reading...

stories.title: Jamaican police officer charged with murder after woman shot during protest
stories.summary: Andrew Wilson appears in court over killing of Latoya Bulgin at protest over a police shooting days earlier Authorities in Jamaica have taken the rare step of charging a police officer with murder after he was accused of shooting a 45-year-old woman in a case that prompted violent protests. According to the Independent Commission of Investigations (Indecom), Constable Andrew Wilson appeared in court on Wednesday and was denied bail. Another hearing is scheduled for mid-June. Continue reading...

stories.title: Top Democrat urges end to secrecy over proposed ICE child detention center
stories.summary: Ron Wyden tells of ‘grave concerns’ over plan, first revealed by Guardian, to hold families at sprawling Louisiana facility The ranking member on the US Senate’s influential finance committee has demanded transparency over a proposed “first-of-its-kind” ICE family and child detention center in Alexandria, Louisiana, citing reporting by the Guardian that first revealed the Trump administration’s plans in March. Senator Ron Wyden, an Oregon Democrat, has written to the project’s contractors and to

stories.title: Trump hits back at 'unpatriotic' vote after House rebukes him over Iran
stories.summary: The lower chamber of Congress passed a measure that seeks to halt further military action, in a vote seen as largely symbolic.

stories.title: EU considers tightening protections for military-age Ukrainians
stories.summary: EU governments are considering restricting temporary protection for military-age Ukrainian men, as member states debate balancing support, integration pressures and Ukraine's manpower needs.

stories.title: Beijing slams US comments on Tiananmen Square crackdown as 'smear' of China
stories.summary: On 4 June 1989, the Chinese government sent troops and tanks to crush protests calling for political reform in and around the central square in the capital Beijing.

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Russia losing on the ground so pivots to air war, say analysts
stories.summary: Minimal gains on battlefield as Kyiv largely halts Moscow’s spring-summer offensive; Ukraine missile maker tests homegrown Patriot alternative. What we know on day 1,562 Russia’s failure to advance on the battlefield is why it is escalating its air raids on major Ukrainian cities , analysts say. The Institute for the Study of War (ISW) thinktank said the strikes were also aimed at distracting from the impact of Ukrainian long-range attacks into Russia . The Finnish Black Bird Group’s latest data

stories.title: Von der Leyen’s AI pick triggers conflict-of-interest criticism
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The appointment of Siemens’ chairman as a European Commission adviser on industrial AI is triggering a backlash in Brussels, weeks after the German engineering giant helped secure a rollback of the EU’s AI rules. “My first reaction was just: Wow,” said Kim van Sparrentak, a Dutch lawmaker who led the work on the AI file for the Greens in the European Parliament.  “They fought hard against AI rules for themselves, they lobby against technological sovereignty, and

stories.title: UK to challenge EU over ‘devastating’ plans to almost halve tariff-free steel import quotas
stories.summary: Business secretary to meet European counterpart on Friday as EU industry leaders worry about retaliatory measures by UK Business live – latest updates The UK business secretary, Peter Kyle, is to raise concerns about EU plans to dramatically reduce tariff-free imports of British steel with its trade commissioner, Maroš Šefčovič, in Brussels on Friday. The UK steel industry has previously warned of “devastating” consequences from the new quota system being planned by the EU, which will cut overal

stories.title: ‘Embarrassing’: pressure on Merz after Germany’s failure to win UN security council seat
stories.summary: Criticism comes from across political spectrum after blow to Friedrich Merz’s government Germany’s unprecedented failure to win one of the rotating seats on the UN security council has prompted an intense round of soul searching in Berlin, and raised questions about its claims to international leadership under Friedrich Merz. The council vote on Wednesday, which elected Austria and Portugal to a two-year term along with Trinidad and Tobago and Zimbabwe, was a blow to Merz’s struggling government

stories.title: Nauru issues rare statement after whistleblower alleges violent threats against Australia’s non-citizens
stories.summary: Nauran government says its citizens are ‘friendly’ after MP spoke of serious threats allegedly made against former Australian detainees Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Nauruan government has issued a rare statement insisting it is a “friendly” and “welcoming” country after a whistleblower alleged “serious threats of physical violence” were made against a group of non-citizens removed there by the Albanese government. The unexpected defence, sent shortly after mid

stories.title: Uproar in France as it emerges suspect in case of missing girl had rape allegations dismissed
stories.summary: Interior minister announces review into handling of the cases after body reportedly found in search for 11-year-old Outrage has erupted in France after it emerged the main suspect in the case of an 11-year-old girl missing since last week had been repeatedly accused of sexually abusing children with no action taken. A body was discovered on Thursday and formal identification was under way, an informed source said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Outrage in Argentina after two teen girls murdered as femicide crisis endures
stories.summary: Country is shaken by the brutal murders of two girls, aged 14 and 17, whose bodies were discovered just days apart Argentina has reacted with fury after the bodies of two murdered teenage girls were found just two days apart. The latest killings underscore the South American country’s enduring femicide crisis despite years of feminist campaigning, and have prompted alarm over the decision to cut support for victims of gender-based violence under the far-right administration of Javier Milei. Poli

stories.title: Senate Republicans vote to block motion to ban Trump’s ‘anti-weaponization’ fund – live
stories.summary: Republicans vote against amendment to kill fund after acting attorney general Todd Blanche told lawmakers earlier this week that DOJ was ‘not moving forward with the fund’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email Donald Trump ’s former national security adviser-turned-foe John Bolton is expected to plead guilty over mishandling classified documents, multiple outlets are reporting. According to CNN, which first reported the news citing three sources, Bolton intends to plead guilty to one count of i

stories.title: German asylum benefits cuts violate EU law, top court rules
stories.summary: In a victory for rejected asylum-seekers, the European Court of Justice has ruled they must be given more than just the bare minimum to survive. But as the EU's new migration pact kicks in, will relief be short-lived?

stories.title: Video shows moment drone hits Kuwait airport terminal
stories.summary: Surveillance footage captured the moment a drone struck a passenger terminal at Kuwait International Airport, killing one person and injuring dozens in the latest escalation involving Iran.

stories.title: Greenland is part of Denmark 'for now,' US Secretary of State Marco Rubio says
stories.summary: The US is reported to have had 17 military facilities and more than 10,000 troops on Greenland at the height of the Cold War.

stories.title: Heavy gunfire in Somali capital as row over election delay escalates
stories.summary: The opposition has called for protests after the president's term in office was extended by a year.

stories.title: Clash at protest against Kushner linked Albania resort project
stories.summary: Video shows security personnel arguing with demonstrators before pushing one protester to the ground and dragging him away. The incident took place during a rally organised by environmental groups opposing a luxury resort project linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of US President Donald Trump.

stories.title: EU backs Pashinyan on the eve of Armenia's defining elections
stories.summary: The EU endorsed Armenia’s PM Nikol Pashinyan and announced a support package to counter Russian sanctions, just days before Armenia’s historic elections. Meanwhile, Pashinyan said he would go to Moscow after the vote and proclaimed that "abandoning Karabakh was my greatest service to Armenia".

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill 11 people in Gaza City, medics say
stories.summary: Women and children were among those killed in the attacks on four residential buildings, which Israel's military says killed four Hamas security officials.

stories.title: One killed and dozens injured in Iranian drone strikes on Kuwait airport
stories.summary: Iran says the attack on Kuwait was in retaliation for earlier US strikes on an Iranian oil tanker and island.

stories.title: Watch: New footage shows moment Iranian drone struck Kuwait airport
stories.summary: One person was killed and more than 60 injured in the Iranian drone strike on Wednesday.

stories.title: Penny Wong says she believes Israeli soldiers sexually assaulted and abused Australian women after Gaza flotilla

Appearing at Senate estimates, Wong said the treatment of the Australian women

stories.title: Bondi attack hero Ahmed al-Ahmed charged with assaulting his father
stories.summary: Police allege the 44-year-old lauded for disarming one of the gunmen in December’s attack put his father in a headlock Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Sydney man who disarmed one of the Bondi terror attackers has been charged with assaulting his father. Ahmed al-Ahmed, 44, is accused of allegedly putting his father in a headlock on 9 March at a house in Bankstown, in the city’s west. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘That’s a bad combination’: why Australia may be in for a slushy snow season
stories.summary: Snow arrives in time for the start of ski season, but climate change and El Niño mean it may not stick around for long, experts say Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast There was optimism across Australian alpine resorts this week as their social media channels filled with footage of snow flurries that arrived just in time for the opening of the ski season this weekend. “We couldn’t be more excited,” said the Instagram account of Perisher , the southern hemisphere’s bigge

stories.title: Missing Sherpa guide found alive on Everest after funeral rites had begun
stories.summary: Climbing support team rescue Hillary Dawa Sherpa almost a week on from when he was last seen A Nepali guide who was believed to have died on Mount Everest has been found crawling to base camp a week after going missing – and after his funeral rites had begun. Dawa Sherpa, also known as Hillary Dawa Sherpa after the famous climber Edmund Hillary, was last seen on 29 May but did not reach base camp with other climbing groups. Continue reading...

stories.title: Hezbollah rejects Israel-Lebanon truce as Trump scrambles to end Iran war
stories.summary: Group calls ceasefire a ‘roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people’, throwing regional peace talks into doubt Hezbollah has rejected a US-brokered ceasefire plan agreed by the Lebanese and Israeli governments, throwing the future of a truce in Lebanon and regional peace negotiations into question. The group’s leader, Naim Qassem, called the plan a “roadmap to annihilate part of the Lebanese people” in a statement on Thursday. Continue reading...

stories.title: Canada endorses embattled marine park’s plan to relocate 30 beluga whales
stories.summary: Beluga whales, which Marineland threatened to euthanize in 2025, will be moved to sanctuaries in Spain or across US Canada and an embattled marine park have reached a tentative deal on the future of 30 beluga whales , ending a saga that has captivated the public and angered animal rights groups. The federal fisheries ministry announced this week that all of Marineland’s belugas would be shipped to either Spain or one of four locations in the US , ending whale captivity in Canada. Continue readin

stories.title: Starmer accuses Musk of trying to ‘whip up division’ in UK over Henry Nowak murder
stories.summary: PM says Britons are ‘reasonable, tolerant people’ and backs MP’s legal action against Grok firm over fake sexualised images UK politics live – latest updates Elon Musk is “interfering in our politics” and attempting to create division, Keir Starmer has said, in a significant toughening of government language about the X owner. stories.title: ‘It is unsustainable’: Reform’s billionaire donors inspire panic in Westminster

stories.summary: Ivanka Trump férje a Vlora-öbölben akar nagyszabású beruházásba kezdeni, természetvédő szervezetek szerint már meg is kezdődtek a munkálatok egy védett tengerparti szakaszon.

stories.title: Andoni Iraola lesz a Liverpool vezetőedzője
stories.summary: A spanyol edző Arne Slotot váltja majd.

stories.title: Bekeményít a LEGO, 12 ezer darabos Sagrada Família készlet érkezik
stories.summary: Ránézésre annyi ideig tart majd összerakni, mint az eredeti épületet.

stories.title: Lassulni fog a világgazdasági növekedés a Fitch szerint Irán miatt
stories.summary: A hitelminősítő 0,2 százalékponttal csökkentette a 2026-ra szóló globális növekedési becslését.

stories.title: Kidnappings, threats and ‘protection fees’: how can Mexico confront rise in deadly extortion?
stories.summary: With corrupt police on the streets and shopkeepers forced to pay gangs, president has vowed to tackle crime that now affects all parts of society It was about 11pm and Luis* was about to get into an Uber to go home when the police car pulled up. One of the officers frisked him and produced two plastic bags with what looked like drugs: one contained some sort of powder, the other little crystals. Luis had never seen them before. Luis, who asked not to use his real name for fear of reprisals, insi

stories.title: UK: Protest erupts over student murder
stories.summary: Protesters have clashed with police in Southampton over the December murder of finance student Henry Nowak. The fatally injured victim was handcuffed by police after his killer accused him of racist abuse.

stories.title: US and Iran exchange new strikes as ceasefire talks falter over Israel's military actions in Lebanon
stories.summary: Iran fired missiles toward Kuwait and Bahrain but failed to hit their targets, with two of those fired at Kuwait falling apart en route, while those aimed at Bahrain were intercepted, the US Central Command said.

stories.title: UK: Police and protesters clash at Southampton rally over student killing
stories.summary: Clashes broke out between protesters and police in Southampton after the release of bodycam footage linked to the fatal stabbing of student Henry Nowak sparked public anger.

stories.title: 11 officers injured in Henry Nowak murder protests in the UK, police say
stories.summary: In a joint statement, a conglomerate of Sikh community groups condemned what it called a "moment of madness by an individual".

stories.title: Drone attack on UAE's Barakah more dangerous than Zaporizhzhia, IAEA chief tells Euronews
stories.summary: Rafael Grossi told Euronews the drone strike on the operating UAE nuclear plant posed greater risks than Zaporizhzhia because its reactors were live at the time of the attack, warning that tonnes of nuclear material could have triggered a radiological accident with "very, very serious consequences".

stories.title: Ukrainian drones hit St Petersburg as Putin's flagship economic forum opens
stories.summary: Several long-range drones struck oil storage facilities near the city, days before Vladimir Putin is due to address the event.

stories.title: At least 21 people, mostly foreign nationals, killed in New Delhi hotel fire, police say
stories.summary: Several of those killed were foreigners, mainly from Central Asia and Africa, the Press Trust of India news agency reported, quoting unnamed officials.

stories.title: Hundreds of Russian strikes across Ukraine kill at least 14 people – video
stories.summary: Russia launched 73 missiles and 656 drones at Ukraine overnight, according to the Ukrainian air force, including eight hypersonic Tsirkon missiles. The main targets were Kyiv, Dnipro, Zaporizhzhia, Poltava and Kharkiv. The Ukrainian president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has asked his US counterpart, Donald Trump, to send Patriot missiles to the country after the Russian attacks killed at least 14 people and injured dozens more Zelenskyy asks Trump to send missiles after Russian strikes across Ukraine

stories.title: DRC Ebola outbreak could have begun as early as January, WHO chief says
stories.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus says the virus ‘had a big head start’ but that the response was catching up The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo could have begun as early as January, the head of the World Health Organization said, giving the virus “a big head start”. Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus also said the response was being hindered by blanket travel restrictions and highlighted high levels of community mistrust and low levels of contact tracing as key concerns. Continue re

stories.title: Wim Wenders withdraws 1975 film featuring 13-year-old Nastassja Kinski topless
stories.summary: German director says he recognises actor should have been better protected during filming of Wrong Move German director Wim Wenders has withdrawn from circulation his 1975 film Wrong Move, because of a scene featuring a child actor topless who was 13 at the time of filming. The director said in a statement released on Wednesday: “Streaming, TV and distribution partners have been instructed to no longer make the film publicly accessible.” Continue reading...

stories.title: Armenia's election offers a choice between Russia and EU
stories.summary: Armenians are set to vote on the country's future geopolitical direction on June 7. The election will be a test of public opinion in the long-standing Russia ally, where the population is increasingly eyeing the EU.

stories.title: Who owns the billions in Germany's dormant bank accounts?
stories.summary: Billions of euros sit untouched in dormant bank accounts in Germany. With heirs unaware and no central register in place, banks and politicians are debating who should ultimately control the money.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia and Russia draw closer amid OPEC upheaval
stories.summary: As Saudi Arabia and Russia deepen cooperation, their combined influence over global oil supply is growing. Could this shift the balance of power in energy markets?

stories.title: Google mosquito army: Scientists say 'we must take action'
stories.summary: Google's Debug research program plans to release millions of sterile mosquitoes to fight species that spread diseases like dengue. How does the method work — and should humans interfere with nature like this?

stories.title: Wim Wenders withdraws film with disputed child nude scene
stories.summary: Actress Nastassja Kinski spent years calling for the removal of a sexualized scene from Wim Wenders' film "The Wrong Move," in which she appeared topless at the age of 13.

stories.title: Massive drug tunnel uncovered beneath US-Mexico border
stories.summary: US authorities released footage of a sophisticated tunnel running beneath the US-Mexico border, saying it was used to smuggle more than $45 million worth of cocaine from Tijuana into California.

stories.title: French artist JR's giant 'cave' installation in Paris appears damaged
stories.summary: Strong winds and heavy rain damaged artist JR’s giant “La Caverne” installation covering Paris’ Pont Neuf bridge, forcing organisers to postpone its public opening scheduled for June 6.

stories.title: A man in Turkey became one of the richest people in the world without realising it
stories.summary: With a balance of around $21.8 billion in his account, Takalo ranks among the world's super rich, but he has been unable to access his money for nearly a month.

stories.title: Foreign nationals among at least 21 killed in Delhi fire
stories.summary: Many victims were South Asians who had travelled to India for treatment or to accompany relatives.

stories.title: Fire tears through New Delhi building, killing at least 18
stories.summary: A fire in a New Delhi building killed at least 18 people and injured several others, with rescue teams pulling dozens from the Malviya Nagar block.

stories.title: Trump confirms calling Netanyahu 'f****** crazy' in telephone row over Lebanon strikes
stories.summary: The comments provide a rare public glimpse into tensions between the two leaders, who have long presented a united front despite occasional disagreements behind closed doors.

stories.title: SOCAR, TotalEnergies and ADNOC lock in Caspian gas supply deal
stories.summary: Azerbaijan's SOCAR signed a long-term deal with TotalEnergies, ADNOC's investment arm XRG and Turkey's BOTAŞ to export gas from the Absheron offshore field from 2029, bolstering the Southern Gas Corridor that Europe has increasingly relied on in recent years.

stories.title: FBI agents fatally shoot man holding hostages in California building, police say
stories.summary: Nearby buildings, including City Hall and the police headquarters, were evacuated and some roads were temporarily closed during the hostage situation.

stories.title: ‘You’re f****** crazy’: Trump clashes with Netanyahu as Lebanon tensions rise
stories.summary: Trump rebukes Benjamin Netanyahu over Lebanon escalation, warning strikes risk isolating Israel and derailing Iran talks, according to reports

stories.title: ‘We don’t have another country to run to’: Kenyans fear US plan for Ebola quarantine site
stories.summary: People from town of potential site for US citizens exposed to Ebola say it puts them at risk in country with no known cases People from a town in central Kenya where the US wants to set up an Ebola quarantine facility for its citizens have strongly criticised the plan, saying they fear it will expose them to the virus and that it is indicative of double standards on the part of the US. “Everybody should be quarantined in their home country. We shouldn’t allow foreigners to bring us diseases,” sa
